mission.title: Install and Configure SlimFaas on Kubernetes
mission.description: SlimFaas is a lightweight, plug-and-play Function-as-a-Service (FaaS) platform for Kubernetes. It scales functions to zero and back up on demand, with built-in pub/sub (sync and async) and a consensus layer for high availability.

steps.title: Clone the SlimFaas repository
steps.description:
Clone the repository to get the deployment manifests:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/AxaFrance/SlimFaas.git
cd SlimFaas/demo
```

steps.title: Deploy RBAC and SlimFaas
steps.description:
Apply the ServiceAccount, Role, RoleBinding, and SlimFaas StatefulSet:
```bash
kubectl apply -f service-account-slimfaas.yml
kubectl apply -f deployment-slimfaas.yml
```
This creates the `slimfaas-demo` namespace, a ConfigMap, a 3-replica StatefulSet, and a ClusterIP service on port 5000.

steps.title: Expose SlimFaas
steps.description:
Expose the service via NodePort (or configure an Ingress):
```bash
kubectl apply -f slimfaas-nodeport.yml
```
SlimFaas will be accessible on NodePort 30021.

steps.title: Deploy sample functions
steps.description:
Deploy the demo functions to test scale-to-zero:
```bash
kubectl apply -f deployment-functions.yml
```
This deploys sample Fibonacci functions (fibonacci1, fibonacci2, fibonacci3) that SlimFaas will scale to zero after idle timeout.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that SlimFaas and the demo functions are running:
```bash
kubectl get statefulset -n slimfaas-demo
kubectl get pods -n slimfaas-demo
kubectl get svc -n slimfaas-demo
```
You should see the slimfaas StatefulSet with 3 ready replicas and the function deployments.

resolution.summary: A successful installation shows the SlimFaas StatefulSet running with 3 replicas in the slimfaas-demo namespace. The service is accessible on port 5000 (ClusterIP) or NodePort 30021. Functions scale to zero after idle and wake on incoming requests.

uninstall.title: Remove SlimFaas Resources
uninstall.description:
Delete all resources and the namespace:
```bash
kubectl delete -f deployment-functions.yml
kubectl delete -f slimfaas-nodeport.yml
kubectl delete -f deployment-slimfaas.yml
kubectl delete -f service-account-slimfaas.yml
```
Note: PersistentVolumeClaims created by the StatefulSet are not automatically deleted. Clean them up:
```bash
kubectl delete pvc --all -n slimfaas-demo
kubectl delete namespace slimfaas-demo
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade SlimFaas
upgrade.description:
Pull the latest manifests and reapply:
```bash
git pull origin main
kubectl apply -f deployment-slimfaas.yml
```
The StatefulSet will perform a rolling update.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
Check the pod logs and events:
```bash
kubectl logs slimfaas-0 -n slimfaas-demo
kubectl describe pod slimfaas-0 -n slimfaas-demo
```

troubleshooting.title: PVC Pending
troubleshooting.description:
SlimFaas uses PersistentVolumeClaims for its consensus layer. If pods are stuck in Pending, check that your cluster has a default StorageClass with available PersistentVolumes:
```bash
kubectl get sc
kubectl get pvc -n slimfaas-demo
```

troubleshooting.title: Functions Not Scaling Up
troubleshooting.description:
If functions remain at 0 replicas after receiving traffic, check SlimFaas logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs slimfaas-0 -n slimfaas-demo
```
Verify the ServiceAccount has the correct RBAC permissions to scale deployments.

prerequisites.description: A Kubernetes cluster with a default StorageClass (for PersistentVolumeClaims), kubectl, and git.
